description:
Claude Code ecosystem configuration.

Note: buddy customization is HM-only (devenv is per-project, buddy is per-user). Use the home-manager programs.claude-code.buddy or ai.claude.buddy option instead.
name: ai.claude

description: Buddy companion customization. When set, fans out to programs.claude-code.buddy which installs an activation script to patch the buddy salt at activation time. See modules/claude-code-buddy/ for details.
name: ai.claude.buddy

description: Preferred lowest stat. Must differ from peak when both are set. null = accept whatever the salt produces.
name: ai.claude.buddy.dump

description: Hat accessory. Must be "none" for common rarity (assertion enforced at module evaluation).
name: ai.claude.buddy.hat

description: Preferred highest stat. null = accept whatever the salt produces. Increases search time ~5x.
name: ai.claude.buddy.peak

description:
Rarity tier. Higher rarities take longer to compute at activation time:

  • common: instant (~180 attempts)
  • uncommon/rare: <1s
  • epic: ~1s
  • legendary: ~1s (or ~30s shiny, minutes shiny+stats)

The salt search is cached by a fingerprint of buddy options

  • claude-code version + userId — only re-runs when something changes.
name: ai.claude.buddy.rarity

description: Rainbow shimmer variant. Significantly increases salt search time (~100x more attempts).
name: ai.claude.buddy.shiny

description: Claude account UUID source. Provide exactly one of text (literal string) or file (path to file read at activation).
type: attribute-tagged union with choices: file, text
name: ai.claude.buddy.userId

description: Path to a file containing the Claude account UUID. Read at activation time, so sops-nix and agenix paths work. Trailing whitespace is stripped.
example: config.sops.secrets."${username}-claude-uuid".path
name: ai.claude.buddy.userId.file

description: Literal Claude account UUID string. Get it from ~/.claude.json under oauthAccount.accountUuid.
name: ai.claude.buddy.userId.text

description: Whether to enable Fan out shared config to Claude Code.
name: ai.claude.enable

description: Whether to enable Fan out shared config to Claude Code.
name: ai.claude.enable

description: Whether to enable Fan out shared config to Copilot CLI.
name: ai.copilot.enable

description: Whether to enable Fan out shared config to Copilot CLI.
name: ai.copilot.enable

description: Shared environment variables for all enabled CLIs.
name: ai.environmentVariables

description: Shared environment variables for all enabled CLIs.
name: ai.environmentVariables

description: Shared instructions with optional path scoping. Body is shared; frontmatter is generated per ecosystem.
name: ai.instructions

description: Shared instructions with optional path scoping. Body is shared; frontmatter is generated per ecosystem.
name: ai.instructions

description: Short description (used by Claude and Kiro frontmatter).
name: ai.instructions.<name>.description

description: Short description (used by Claude and Kiro frontmatter).
name: ai.instructions.<name>.description

description:
File path globs this instruction applies to. null = always loaded. Translated per ecosystem:

  • Claude: paths: frontmatter
  • Kiro: inclusion: fileMatch + fileMatchPattern:
  • Copilot: applyTo: glob
name: ai.instructions.<name>.paths

description:
File path globs this instruction applies to. null = always loaded. Translated per ecosystem:

  • Claude: paths: frontmatter
  • Kiro: inclusion: fileMatch + fileMatchPattern:
  • Copilot: applyTo: glob
name: ai.instructions.<name>.paths

description: Whether to enable Fan out shared config to Kiro CLI.
name: ai.kiro.enable

description: Whether to enable Fan out shared config to Kiro CLI.
name: ai.kiro.enable

description: Typed LSP server definitions with explicit packages. Transformed to per-ecosystem JSON (with full store paths) during fanout.
example: { nixd = { package = pkgs.nixd; extensions = ["nix"]; }; marksman = { package = pkgs.marksman; extensions = ["md"]; }; }
name: ai.lspServers

description: Typed LSP server definitions with explicit packages. Transformed to per-ecosystem JSON (with full store paths) during fanout. Each CLI writes the result to its own config path.
example: { nixd = { package = pkgs.nixd; extensions = ["nix"]; }; marksman = { package = pkgs.marksman; extensions = ["md"]; }; }
name: ai.lspServers

description: Arguments to pass to the LSP binary.
name: ai.lspServers.<name>.args

description: Arguments to pass to the LSP binary.
name: ai.lspServers.<name>.args

description: Binary name within the package (defaults to the attribute name).
name: ai.lspServers.<name>.binary

description: Binary name within the package (defaults to the attribute name).
name: ai.lspServers.<name>.binary

description: File extensions this server handles (without leading dots).
name: ai.lspServers.<name>.extensions

description: File extensions this server handles (without leading dots).
name: ai.lspServers.<name>.extensions

description: Shared skills (directory paths). Identical format across ecosystems. Injected at mkDefault priority so per-ecosystem config wins.
name: ai.skills

description: Shared skills (directory paths). Identical format across ecosystems. Injected at mkDefault priority so per-CLI skills win.
name: ai.skills

type: attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"
name: copilot.agents

description: Environment variables for Copilot (merged into devenv env).
name: copilot.environmentVariables

type: attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"
name: copilot.instructions

type: attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"
name: kiro.agents

description: Environment variables for Kiro (merged into devenv env).
name: kiro.environmentVariables

type: attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"
name: kiro.hooks

type: attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"
name: kiro.steering

type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.copilot-cli.agents

description: Config directory relative to HOME. Override via COPILOT_HOME.
name: programs.copilot-cli.configDir

description: Extra environment variables exported when launching copilot CLI.
example: { COPILOT_AUTO_UPDATE = "false"; COPILOT_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4"; }
name: programs.copilot-cli.environmentVariables

type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.copilot-cli.instructions

example: { typescript = { command = "typescript-language-server"; args = ["--stdio"]; fileExtensions = {".ts" = "typescript";}; }; }
name: programs.copilot-cli.lspServers

description: JSON settings merged into ~/.copilot/config.json on activation. Runtime-mutated keys (trusted_folders, etc.) are preserved. Known keys are typed; unknown keys are accepted via freeformType.
example: { autoUpdates = false; model = "claude-sonnet-4"; theme = "dark"; }
name: programs.copilot-cli.settings

type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.copilot-cli.skills

type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.kiro-cli.agents

type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.kiro-cli.hooks

example: { nix = { command = "nixd"; args = []; }; }
name: programs.kiro-cli.lspServers

description: JSON settings merged into ~/.kiro/settings/cli.json on activation. Runtime-mutated keys are preserved. Known keys are typed; unknown keys are accepted via freeformType.
example: { chat = { defaultModel = "claude-sonnet-4"; enableThinking = true; }; telemetry.enabled = false; }
name: programs.kiro-cli.settings

type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.kiro-cli.skills

description: Global steering .md files for ~/.kiro/steering/. Should include YAML frontmatter with inclusion mode (auto, always, manual, fileMatch).
type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
name: programs.kiro-cli.steering

description: Enable dynamic toolsets discovery. When false (default), all tools from configured toolsets are available at startup. When true, only meta-tools are advertised initially and toolsets must be enabled at runtime — requires client support for tools/list_changed notifications.

description: Filter public repository content by push access. Only shows repos the token can push to.
name: services.mcp-servers.servers.github-mcp.settings.lockdownMode

description: Cache duration for repository access checks (e.g. "5m", "1h"). Server default is used when unset.

description: Individual tool names to enable (additive with toolsets).

description: Toolset groups to enable. Each group activates a related set of tools.
name: services.mcp-servers.servers.github-mcp.settings.toolsets
